entities.name: Bangkok Chain Hospital Public Company Limited

financial.note: SSO holds ~3.57% stake; more critically, SSO patients provide ~30-35% of total revenue. 2026 wage ceiling hikes are a direct positive catalyst for per-head revenue.
Provides long-term credit facilities for hospital expansions; ~33% of debt is floating rate, making interest expense sensitive to BoT policy shifts.
VIBHA holds ~1.88% of BCH; financial performance and valuation multiples often correlate due to shared board interests and sector sentiment.

supply_chain.note: Primary distributors for pharmaceutical products and medical supplies; rising drug costs directly squeeze hospital gross margins.
Key supplier of healthcare technical products and imaging equipment; sensitive to THB depreciation on imported technology.

competitors.note: Direct rival in the Social Security (SSO) segment and suburban mid-tier market.
Competes for premium international patients via World Medical Hospital brand.
Competes for high-end medical tourism (Middle East/CLMV), specifically Kuwaiti and Cambodian fly-in traffic.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Direct impact on cash flow from the fixed-payment patient segment; the 2026 ceiling increase to 17,500 THB is a primary revenue driver.
A weaker THB makes Thai healthcare cheaper for international fly-in patients, though it increases the cost of imported medical supplies (Inverse impact on OPEX).
Hospitals are high-intensity utility consumers; rising energy prices significantly impact the operating margins of 24/7 facilities.
Affects the cost of servicing debentures used for 'Kasemrad Suvarnabhumi' and other regional expansions.
